# dynamoip

> Give local dev servers real domain names and trusted HTTPS — reachable from your network or the entire internet.

dynamoip is a reverse proxy and DNS automation tool for local development and self-hosted services. It runs on your machine, manages DNS records, obtains or generates TLS certificates, and proxies HTTPS traffic to your local services by hostname. With Max mode, it can also expose services publicly via Cloudflare Tunnel — no open ports, no firewall changes needed.

## Three modes

**Max mode** — exposes services to the public internet via Cloudflare Tunnel. No sudo required. Cloudflare handles TLS. `cloudflared` is installed automatically on first run. Activated by adding `"tunnel": true` to config alongside `baseDomain`.

**Pro mode** — LAN access using a real domain you own. dynamoip sets A records in Cloudflare and obtains a Let's Encrypt wildcard certificate via DNS-01. Every device on the LAN trusts HTTPS out of the box. Requires sudo to bind to port 443.

**Quick mode** — LAN access with no domain needed. Uses mDNS (`.local` hostnames) and mkcert. Other devices need the CA cert installed once. Requires sudo.

## Installation

```bash
npm install --save-dev dynamoip   # recommended: dev dependency
npm install -g dynamoip           # or global
```

Requires Node.js >= 14. Runs on macOS, Linux, and Windows (Windows via Docker only).

## Config file: dynamoip.config.json

```json
{
  "baseDomain": "yourdomain.com",
  "domains": {
    "app": 3000,
    "api": 4000
  },
  "tunnel": true
}
```

- `baseDomain` — domain root (Pro + Max mode). Can be apex or subdomain.
- `domains` — map of subdomain name to local port.
- `tunnel` — set `true` to enable Max mode (Cloudflare Tunnel). Requires `baseDomain`.
- `port` — proxy listen port. Defaults: 8080 in Max mode, 443 in Pro/Quick, 80 in HTTP mode.

Omit `baseDomain` for Quick mode. Omit `tunnel` (or set `false`) for Pro mode.

## Environment variables

| Variable       | Required                        | Description |
|----------------|---------------------------------|-------------|
| `CF_API_TOKEN` | Pro + Max mode                  | Cloudflare API token. Pro: needs `Zone:DNS:Edit`. Max: needs `Zone:DNS:Edit` + `Account:Cloudflare Tunnel:Edit`. Create at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens |
| `CF_EMAIL`     | No                              | Email for Let's Encrypt expiry notifications (Pro mode only) |
| `LAN_IP`       | Docker on macOS/Win (Pro/Quick) | Override LAN IP auto-detection. Not needed in Max mode. |
| `TARGET_HOST`  | Docker on macOS/Win (Max mode)  | Host the proxy forwards to. Set `host.docker.internal` so the container reaches services on the host machine. Defaults to `localhost`. |

Place these in a `.env` file next to `dynamoip.config.json`. Never commit `.env`.

## CLI

```
dynamoip [options]

--config <path>   Config file path (default: ./dynamoip.config.json)
--port <n>        Override proxy port
--no-ssl          Plain HTTP only
--help            Show help
```

Max mode does not require sudo. Pro and Quick mode require sudo to bind to ports 80 and 443:

```bash
# Max mode — no sudo
npm run proxy:live

# Pro / Quick mode
sudo npm run dev:proxy
sudo pnpm run dev:proxy
sudo yarn dev:proxy
```

Always run via your package manager (not bare `sudo dynamoip`) so the binary is found in `node_modules/.bin`.

## Ready output

dynamoip labels every URL by exposure level:

```
[PUBLIC]  https://app.yourdomain.com    ← Max mode — internet accessible
[LAN]     https://app.yourdomain.com    ← Pro mode — LAN only
[LAN]     https://app.local             ← Quick mode — LAN only
```

## How Max mode works

```
Any device (LAN or internet)
  → https://app.yourdomain.com
  → DNS CNAME → {tunnel-id}.cfargotunnel.com
  → Cloudflare edge (terminates TLS)
  → cloudflared daemon (outbound tunnel, on your machine)
  → http://127.0.0.1:8080
  → dynamoip proxy (localhost only)
  → localhost:3000
```

- Tunnel credentials stored in `~/.localmap/tunnels/{id}.json` (mode 0600)
- Tunnel config rewritten each startup so ingress stays in sync with `domains`
- On restart, existing tunnel is reused — DNS is unchanged
- `cloudflared` auto-installed: Homebrew on macOS, `sudo curl` to `/usr/local/bin` on Linux

## Running in Docker

**Max mode (macOS/Windows):** set `TARGET_HOST=host.docker.internal`. No `LAN_IP` needed. Install `cloudflared` in the Dockerfile (see `docs/docker.md`).

**Max mode (Linux):** use `network_mode: host`. No extra env vars needed.

**Pro/Quick mode (macOS/Windows):** set `LAN_IP` to your machine's LAN IP. Use `${LAN_IP:-}` in docker-compose to pass it through.

**Pro/Quick mode (Linux):** use `network_mode: host` and leave `LAN_IP` unset.

See [docs/docker.md](docs/docker.md) for full Docker guide with compose examples.

## Cert and tunnel storage

```
~/.localmap/
├── certs/                    Pro mode — Let's Encrypt cert cache
│   ├── wildcard.pem
│   ├── wildcard-key.pem
│   └── meta.json
└── tunnels/                  Max mode — tunnel credentials
    ├── {tunnel-id}.json      (mode 0600, created once)
    └── config.yml            (rewritten each startup)
```

Pro mode certs auto-renew 30 days before expiry with hot-reload (no restart needed). Delete `~/.localmap/certs/` to force a fresh certificate.

## Project layout

```
bin/dynamoip.js       Entry point — argument parsing, startup orchestration
src/config.js         Config loading, validation, .env parsing
src/cloudflare.js     Cloudflare API — zone lookup, A/CNAME records, ACME TXT
src/tunnel.js         Cloudflare Tunnel — create/reuse, credentials, cloudflared
src/acme.js           Let's Encrypt DNS-01, cert cache, renewal scheduler
src/certs.js          mkcert integration (Quick mode)
src/proxy.js          HTTP/HTTPS reverse proxy, WebSocket support
src/mdns.js           mDNS — dns-sd (macOS), avahi (Linux)
src/ip.js             LAN IP detection (env override + auto-detect)
src/cleanup.js        Signal handling, child process cleanup
docs/tunnel.md        Max mode setup guide
docs/docker.md        Docker support (macOS, Windows, Linux)
docs/local-development.md   Using dynamoip as a dev dependency
```

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md) — full setup guide and reference
- [docs/tunnel.md](docs/tunnel.md) — Max mode (Cloudflare Tunnel) setup
- [docs/docker.md](docs/docker.md) — Docker support
- [docs/local-development.md](docs/local-development.md) — dev dependency setup
